messages.content: You are a helpful AI assistant. You provide clear, accurate answers to questions about any topic. Be concise but thorough.
Who was the Greek god of the sea?

Poseidon was the Greek god of the sea, earthquakes, storms, and horses. He was one of the Twelve Olympians and brother to Zeus and Hades. His weapon was the trident, which he used to create springs, cause earthquakes, and stir up tidal waves. He was widely worshipped by sailors and fishermen across the ancient Greek world.
What about his wife? Was she also a sea deity?

Poseidon's wife was Amphitrite, a sea goddess and one of the Nereids (the fifty daughters of Nereus and Doris). While she was indeed a sea deity, she was generally less prominent in Greek mythology than Poseidon himself. According to myth, she initially fled from Poseidon's advances but was eventually persuaded to marry him, often by the dolphin god Delphin. Together they had a son, Triton, who was a merman and served as his father's herald.
Did they have any other children besides Triton?

Yes, Poseidon had several other children besides Triton, though Amphitrite was primarily known as the mother of Triton. Poseidon was famously fertile and had many children with various partners. With Amphitrite, some sources mention a daughter, Rhode (the nymph of the island of Rhodes). With the goddess Demeter, he fathered the horse Arion. With Medusa, he fathered Pegasus (the winged horse) and Chrysaor. He also had several human children including Theseus (with Aethra) and Polyphemus the Cyclops (with the nymph Thoosa).
